_comment: Specialized kiro-cli agent skeleton. Maps to the 7-pattern recipe in SKILL.md. Filled-in example: .kiro/agents/autofix.json + .kiro/shared/autofix-system.md. Delete _comment fields before use.
name: REPLACE-agent-name
description: REPLACE — one sentence: what this agent does and the single job it owns.
_pattern_1_role: ROLE — the prompt file declares what it does AND what it must NOT do. Keep it surgical.
_pattern_3_tools: TOOLS — minimum viable set. Prefer few atomic-outcome tools. Do not use '*' unless the agent genuinely needs everything.
_pattern_3_denied: TOOLS — denylist dangerous paths mechanically. The agent cannot edit what it should not touch. Tune to this agent's blast radius.
_pattern_2_input: INPUT — resources load steering + AGENTS.md (progressive disclosure). Structured task context comes via env vars documented in the system prompt, not inline here.
_pattern_4_hooks: HOOKS — enforce mechanically what the agent must never skip. matcher uses INTERNAL tool names: fs_write, fs_read, execute_bash, str_replace (NOT write/read/shell). See references/kiro-config-guide.md gotcha #1.

agentSpawn._why: LIFECYCLE — clear stale verification state at session start.

preToolUse._why: SCOPE — block dangerous ops the denylist cannot express (e.g. git mutations).
preToolUse.command: bash -c 'CMD=$(cat | jq -r .tool_input.command 2>/dev/null); echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "git +(commit|push|reset|rebase)" && { echo BLOCKED >&2; exit 2; } || exit 0'

postToolUse._why: VERIFICATION feedforward — auto-format on write so the agent never spends tokens on whitespace; record modified files.
postToolUse.command: bash -c 'FILE=$(cat | jq -r .tool_input.path 2>/dev/null); case "$FILE" in *.rs) cargo fmt -- "$FILE" 2>/dev/null||true;; esac; echo "$FILE" >> "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/AGENT-modified.txt"'

postToolUse._why: VERIFICATION — flag that sensors ran, for the stop gate.
postToolUse.command: bash -c 'CMD=$(cat | jq -r .tool_input.command 2>/dev/null); echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "cargo (clippy|test)|npm (run build|test)" && touch "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/AGENT-sensors.txt"||true'

stop._why: VERIFICATION gate — refuse to finish if files changed but no sensor ran. This makes 'claim done without verifying' impossible, not improbable.
stop.command: bash -c 'M="${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/AGENT-modified.txt"; S="${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/AGENT-sensors.txt"; if [ -s "$M" ] && [ ! -f "$S" ]; then echo "{\"decision\":\"block\",\"reason\":\"Files modified but no verification sensor ran. Run the relevant sensors before stopping.\"}"; else rm -f "$M" "$S" 2>/dev/null; fi'
